title: Sickle Cell Disease (SCD) portfolio case-study witness (v1)
description: In-silico simulator-consistency witness for the sickle cell disease (SCD) portfolio case study. SCD is the rare disease with FDA-approved drugs across MULTIPLE distinct modality classes — making it a uniquely rich axis-mapping showcase. The schema separates THREE structurally-distinct categories: (1) `in_scope_drugs[]` — FDA-approved CDER small molecules whose modality maps onto in-repo axes (voxelotor — uniquely DUAL-mapped PPI + ALLOSTERIC); (2) `cder_in_scope_no_axis_mapping[]` — FDA-approved CDER small molecules that fall inside the criterion #4 scope but whose mechanism does NOT map onto any current in-repo axis (hydroxyurea, L-glutamine); (3) `not_in_scope_drugs[]` — FDA-approved drugs whose regulatory jurisdiction is CBER (gene/cell therapy or biologic mAb), excluded by criterion #4 drug-only/CDER discipline (Casgevy, Lyfgenia, crizanlizumab). Voxelotor's DUAL axis mapping (PPI primary + ALLOSTERIC secondary) is supported by a list-of-axes structure per drug, distinct from prior single-axis portfolios. NEVER a therapeutic / clinical / efficacy / regulatory / portfolio-recommendation claim (g8 / f2). NOT the deferred 200-disease re-mapping.

in_scope_drugs.description: FDA-approved CDER small molecules that map cleanly onto one or more in-repo axes. For SCD this is the single entry voxelotor, with TWO axis_mapping entries (PPI primary + ALLOSTERIC secondary).

cder_in_scope_no_axis_mapping.description: FDA-approved CDER small molecules whose regulatory category sits INSIDE the hexa-bio criterion #4 drug-only/CDER scope, but whose mechanism does NOT cleanly map onto any current in-repo axis. Recording these honestly is the point: the portfolio acknowledges where the axis tree does not yet cover an FDA-approved CDER drug for this disease, instead of either inventing a forced axis or silently omitting the drug.

not_in_scope_drugs.description: FDA-approved drugs whose regulatory jurisdiction is CBER (gene/cell therapy or biologic mAb), excluded by criterion #4 drug-only/CDER discipline. Recorded but NOT modeled (reported_not_run=true; axis=null).

axis_mapping_entry.description: A single axis mapping. Voxelotor uniquely carries TWO of these (PPI primary + ALLOSTERIC secondary).

role.description: Voxelotor's PPI mapping is primary (it disrupts HbS-HbS polymer contact); the ALLOSTERIC mapping is secondary (it allosterically shifts Hb's R/T state — pure MWC).

axis_mappings.description: List of axis mappings. Voxelotor uniquely carries TWO (PPI primary + ALLOSTERIC secondary) — the dual-mapping pattern is a structural feature of the schema, distinct from prior single-axis portfolios.

cder_no_axis_drug.description: FDA-approved CDER small molecule INSIDE the criterion #4 scope whose mechanism does NOT map onto any current in-repo axis. Honestly listed, not modeled.

not_in_scope_drug.description: FDA-approved drug whose regulatory jurisdiction is CBER (gene/cell therapy or biologic mAb), excluded by criterion #4 drug-only/CDER discipline. Recorded but NOT modeled.
